LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER LINDA POINTER'S HEARING ON PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD AND ORDER REHEARING THE CONTROVERSY. PETITIONER PRESENTED THE COURT WITH A COPY OF THE MOVING PAPERS. THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUED THE MATTER TO DECEMBER 6, 2011. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-06-2011 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT FILED BY RESPONDENTS
PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD FILED BY PETITIONER POINTER, LINDA L AS TO RESPONDENT ORACLE CORPORATION ORACLE AMERICA, INC. JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED
ORDER DENYING PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-22-2011 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER LINDA POINTER'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD AND ORDER REHEARING THE CONTROVERSY IS DENIED. PETITIONER FAILS TO SUSTAIN HER BURDEN OF SHOWING ANY OF THE GROUNDS SET FORTH IN CCP 1286.2. THE RECORD ON THIS MOTION SHOWS THAT PETITIONER RECEIVED A FAIR HEARING AND ARBITRATOR WAS NOT BIASED AGAINST PETITIONER NOR DID HE FAIL TO ACCOMMODATE ANY MEDICALLY SUBSTANTIATED REQUEST BY PETITIONER. PETITIONER'S ARGUMENT THAT AAA'S ASSERTED LACK OF A PUBLISHED POLICY TO ACCOMMODATE PERSONS WITH DISABILITIES REQUIRES VACATION OF THE AWARD IS UNSUPPORTED BY ANY AUTHORITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
POS ON PROPOSED JUDGMENT FILED BY RESPONDENT ORACLE AMERICA, INC.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT RESPONDENT ORACLE CORPORATION ORACLE AMERICA, INC. RECOVER FROM PETITIONER POINTER, LINDA L RESPONDENTS SHALL RECOVER COSTS OF SUIT. SEE SCANNED DOC
MEMORANDUM OF COSTS AND DISBURSEMENTS, $820.00 TOTAL COSTS, MATURE DATE JAN-25-2012, FILED BY PLAINTIFF ORACLE CORPORATION ORACLE AMERICA, INC.

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Order Rehearing The Controversy
Off Calendar Dec-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - LINDA L. POINTER, TEL. NO. 342-9855, PETITIONER APPEARING IN PRO PER ACCOMPANIED BY LAURA MACKIE (LIFE COACH);
ATTY. BALDWIN J. LEE & ATTY. JENNIE LEE OF ALLEN MATKINS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 273-7446 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER LINDA POINTER'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Order Rehearing The Controversy is Denied. Petitioner fails to sustain her burden of showing any of the grounds set forth in
CCP 1286.2. The record on this motion shows that petitioner received a fair hearing and arbitrator was not biased against petitioner nor did he fail to accommodate any medically substantiated request by petitioner. Petitioner's argument that AAA's asserted lack of a published policy to accommodate persons with disabilities requires vacation of the award is unsupported by any authority. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK

Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Order Rehearing The Controversy
Off Calendar Nov-22-2011 Continued To Dec-06-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER LINDA POINTER'S HEARING ON PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD AND ORDER REHEARING THE CONTROVERSY.
Appearances: Linda Pointer (342.9855) petitioner and moving party appearing in propria persona with Laura Mackie (life coach); Baldwin J. Lee of Allen Matkins (273.7446) for respondent and opposing party.
Petitioner presented the court with a copy of the moving papers. The court vacated its tentative ruling and continued the matter to December 6, 2011. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288
